records.ety: [L. creatio: cf. F. cr<?/ation. See Create.]

defs: The act of creating or causing to exist. Specifically, the act of bringing the universe or this world into existence.
quotes: From the creation to the general doom.
As when a new particle of matter dotn begin to exist, in rerum natura, which had before no being; and this we call creation.

defs: That which is created; that which is produced or caused to exist, as the world or some original work of art or of the imagination; nature.
quotes: We know that the whole creation groaneth.
A dagger of the mind, a false creation.
Choice pictures and creations of curious art.

defs: The act of constituting or investing with a new character; appointment; formation.
quotes: An Irish peer of recent creation.
